title: Tera 18/8 Stainless Steel Mortar and Pestle with Brush,Pill Crusher,Spice Grinder,Herb Bowl,Pesto Powder
description:
About this item
SOLID RUSTPROOF MATERIAL: Both pestle and mortar are made of food grade 18/8 stainless steel. Not only are they rustproof, it is also resistant to damage and corrosion by acidic food. Small compact mortar, about the size of the average palm - whereas one could pick it up with one hand, capacity 250ml
DURABLE AND STABLE: Made of solid metal, the pestle feels substantial in your hand and the mortar sits securely on your kitchen counter. Padding on the bottom makes it stay put even more.
FOOD-SAFE: Because stainless steel does not hold residue or leftover odor, all it takes is a thorough rinsing under the tap to get it ready for next use.
MESSY NOT: Translucent lid allows you to gauge your strikes and keeps the contents in the bowl, not over counter top.
ALL YOUR GRINDING NEEDS: This grinding setup will accommodate just about anyone’s grinding needs. From medical pills, to herbs and spices; from the pharmacy to your kitchen.
reviews_5s:
I’ve tried other products for crushing medication, and this one is by far the best. The quality and heavy weight make it feel substantial and well-made, and I can tell it’s going to last for years. It's not just a functional tool but also a durable one that I can rely on.

It doesn’t retain any residue or odors, so a quick rinse under the tap is all it takes to get it ready for the next use. This is particularly convenient for frequent use, as it saves a lot of time and effort.

Conclusion
Overall, the Tera Stainless Steel Mortar & Pestle is a high-quality, reliable product. Its rustproof and durable construction, combined with its stability and ease of cleaning, make it a great choice for anyone. Whether for culinary purposes or crushing medication, this set stands out for its performance and longevity.I use this to grind up my pets’ supplements into a powder, and it is super easy to use and works so much better than squashing their pills with the back of a spoon! Great value for the quality. The contents don’t really stick to the bowl, not as much as I thought they would, any that does stick can be easily brushed off with the brush it comes with. It’s ergonomically friendly for what it is.I love it! It fits my needs exactly! One of my responsibilities as a school nurse for a special needs student is to grind medication in pill form into a fine powder that I then dilute and medicate my student through a gastric tube. The principle is the same for medicating anyone who can’t swallow pills, or even for medicating your pet.

I love the smooth texture of the inside of the bowl with concentric rings edged into it. The edging helps reduce the effort of crushing the pill and the smoothness helps keep too much of the powder from getting stuck in the bowl. They also provide a little brush to help sweep out any remaining powder so there is no loss at all! Before I have only used marble sets and the natural texture of the stone traps too much of the powder and doesn’t allow as fine a grind.

This set is made of stainless steel. It is heavy duty and feels very sturdy and durable. There is a rubberized ring on the bottom to keep it from shifting in place during use. Cleanup is a cinch.
The only reason that I gave it a lower score on giftability is that I think most people are looking for something for use in the kitchen with herbs or other cooking needs. I think that natural stone would be preferred for that.My wife is from Puerto Rico and has always used a stone/marble Mortar and Pestle. I am just a white boy that thought salt and pepper were "spices" before I met her. The problem with the stone/marble ones was that the pestle would get broken and we would have to buy a new one. (I think she gets aggressive and takes out her frustrations while grinding and the pestle can't handle it) She was pessimistic when she saw this but tried it anyway. She liked it! It is very sturdy, heavy and can take quite a beating. It also cleans up easily. The plastic cover keeps everything inside if you use it (wife does not use it) The little wife would never admit that this is better than the ones in PR but I think she might think that. Don't tell anyone in Puerto Rico about my wife liking this. I wouldn't want her to lose her Puerto Rican card!I like the heavy weight cup and this product is real stainless steel, washable. Very sturdy.Using this to crush meds my cat needs to take, daily, for her health conditions. Super sturdy. Perfect size for meds. Clean appearance and easy to clean. Steel material makes it easy to get all contents out cleanly. Worth it.This product is incredibly stable and quick and easy to use. The brush work’s fantastic to get every last particle of those precious strands! If you cook frequently with saffron you know Persian dishes require it to be high quality and in contaminated by other aromas or residue and much of it is easily lost in a marble or wood mortar and pestle. I had a brass one years ago but I lost it and it was high maintenance. This is easy to clean and I didn’t even need to use the lid for crushing, the pestle is heavy duty and crushes the strands on contact with minimal pressure applied and a quick circular motion will grind it to a fine dust in seconds. I’m about to test it on green cardamom seeds and then peppercorn and update my review as necessary. 😘🤌🏻 don’t hesitate just get it! I got the smaller size and may also get the larger one for pesto!Works well for medicationI used natural medicines which come in the form of very hard and tiny pills. The pill crusher I am throwing away made it impossible to pulverize the pills. Then I thought about a mortar and pestle and turned to Amazon. There are many mortar and pestles from which to choose, but the one that caught my attention is the stainless steel combo with two lids and a brush. The lid is fantastic because it keeps the pills inside the mortar as I smash them into a powder. I LOVE IT!
August 8, 2024 - I just read some of the four-star reviews and had to add to my "opinion." I have two lids. How that happened I consider a blessing. As to the lids not being clear, I have no problem removing the lid to check the progress of smashing those pills. Easy peasy. That brush is awesome. And the weight is great as well. Solid, sturdy. It took me by surprise but I adapted to it. I am no longer frustrated and dealing with a hand that kept getting bruised trying to use the pill crusher that went into the trash. Happy day!Pretty impressed with this mortar & pestle combination. I remembered my Mother having a marble set of these when I was a kid -- sometimes she'd use it with herbs and seasonings for cooking. I didn't really have an immediate need for one when I purchased this set, just some vague ideas that I could use it for.

Now that I've had it for some time, there's some features that I think make this really nice: for one, it being metal really helps with cleanup -- depending on what you're using it for, it'll most definitely need a scrub afterward. The metal makes this easy, and also gives it a really nice weight (both items). The weight makes the process of using it feel really sturdy.

One thing I really didn't pay too much attention to originally (because my mother's never had one), but now really, really appreciate about this, is that it comes with the set of plastic covers to reduce spillage over the sides. This has been a lifesaver -- without it, you'd have to be really careful with your grinding, but instead you can just pop one of these covers and go to work without that issue at all.

The only downside that I can really think of is that it's a little on the smaller side -- great for most uses, but sometimes feel a larger mortar & pestle would have been a little easier to use. Oh well, this works perfectly for my needs, and I don't have to go out of my way to find a spot to keep it when in storage.

All-in-all, completely content with this purchase, very convenient item to have in the kitchen!
reviews_1s: Trusted this to be good quality due to the high rating. And I rarely have to return things that I order. Washed it thoroughly and then tried to grind some course sea salt. It turned the sea salt dark gray and it gave off a metallic odor. Washed it again and repeated the process, but it had the same results. The salt also left visible spotting in the surface of the mortar as well as the pestle. If it is real stainless (which I highly doubt), it’s far from food grade quality. I would not want it in contact with my food.The only reason I purchased this particular product was because the image included a mini sweet brush when the product arrived that was not inside the box.Very difficult to pound garlic b/c it's so shallow. Waste of money, sadly.I bought this for primarily for crushing garlic and ginger with the hope that it would be more easier to clean up than a garlic crusher. Unfortunately, it didn't do the job. The steel doesn't grip to the garlic and ginger pieces. (I'm wondering if the stone (granite/marble) equivalent of this would work better(?)) It is a well-made, sturdy and good-looking tool but it's useless. I'm guessing it might work for crushing soft stuff like bananas and guacamole but I already have a potato masher for that.I'm sorry to say that when I went to hand wash it now, there are little rust stains. I wish I had examined it first in daylight. I just assumed it would be fine because my 18/8 utensils never get rusty, so I'm now skeptical that this product is 18/8 stainless steel. I am returning it immediately. This is so disappointing because, it has a cover and impossible to break. I wanted to love it.Cheap crap. Metal started to chip off from trying to grind some things. Then after it got a bit wet, it started to rust in those spots.In a word: GROSS. Purchased for a family member who needs to crush medical pills for proper dosage. Washed and dried it before use. Seemed great until I poured the crushed medicine out and realized the white pill was now grayish color. I washed and dried it again thinking I just not have done a good enough job and tested it with baking soda and yet again, came out a grayish metal tone. What a waste.I bought this stainless steel mortar/pestle to hand crush rock. After one day of use the pestle opened and revealed it's sand fill. The mortar is filled with concrete to give weight and got a crack on the middle after switching to a hammer due to the pestle failure. This product is not sturdy enough to be used for minerals, stick to plants with this.Tried to crush garlic, but it’s not deep enough! The plastic cover broke after a couple of times. The garlic doesn’t really crush well as you can’t move up and down enough due to the plastic cover. Too shallow or not deep enough! And if you don’t use the plastic cover all the garlic will be on the floor 😬Product arrived and looked like it had been opened before. There was a ton of sand/dirt in the mortar.
